sections.description: The horizon is mapped before the tide is trusted.
The same contour repeats with more confidence and more tide under it.
The atlas stays open, but the water goes dark beneath it.
The line stretches farther out and starts to read as weather.
The motif arrives back on land without losing the pull underneath it.
A single horizon line closes the map and restarts the tide.

insights.message: Counterline beacon returns in the sounding lines and the shoreline return.
86 symbolic timing placements were normalized into absolute beat events.

rhythm.summary: 100% of note events carry rhythm roles, 100% anchor downbeat presence, 0 offbeat-only bars, cadence contract satisfied
insights: Rhythmic bar roles cover 20 of 20 bars.
Anchor voice articulates the downbeat in 100% of its active bars.
Every active bar retains at least one strong-beat articulation.
Cadence contract satisfied at bar 20, beat 1.

issues.message: Section "undertow-seal" is marked as cadence but only 29% of its notes land on strong beats.
